data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-52469547">
    <title>runtime fallback removal + audit-side migration</title>
    <description>
Bead 3 of 4 children under epic ddx-3562c48c. Depends on bead 2 (ddx-908a6d5d). May run in parallel with bead 4.

Strip remaining ~/.ddx/plugins runtime reads; thread Force through update; audit-side path resolution.

PERSONA FALLBACK:
- DELETE cli/internal/persona/loader.go:64 fallback to ~/.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas.
- Missing-config error message: 'DDx config not found; run "ddx init" to bootstrap project'.

AUDIT (cli/internal/registry/audit.go):
- Lines 104, 336, 339: stop ExpandHome on entry.Files. Resolve paths relative to projectRoot.
- Pre-migration entries with ~/... paths uninstall as no-ops (existing os.Remove on missing path is tolerated).
- Covered by TestUninstall_StaleHomeEntriesAreNoOp.

GRAPHQL:
- DELETE cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:746-759 (pluginRootCandidates). Resolver returns project-rooted paths only.
- Update affected resolver tests.

UPDATE COMMAND:
- cli/cmd/update.go: plumb skills.Options{Force: true} into skills.Install for the --force path.
- TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks: project starts with symlinks under .agents/skills/ pointing to ~/.ddx/plugins/, ddx update --force replaces with real files.

WORKTREE FALLBACK (gated decision):
- NEW TestExecuteBead_WorktreeFromPreMigrationCommit: create worktree from a commit predating migration; run skill-resolution path.
- IF the test passes WITHOUT cli/internal/agent/worktree_skills.go:106-114 resolveBrokenSkillLink, DELETE the fallback in this bead.
- IF the test fails, KEEP the fallback as a no-op for new installs and add a code comment + doc note.

DOCTOR:
- cli/cmd/doctor.go: when symlinks exist under <projectRoot>/.agents/skills/ or .claude/skills/, doctor exits non-zero. stderr contains 'symlink detected under .agents/skills/' (and same for .claude/skills/). Recommendation message ends with 'run: ddx update --force'.
- TestDoctor_FlagsLegacySymlinks asserts exit code and stderr substrings.

OUT OF SCOPE: .gitignore, docs (bead 4). State.json relocation (separate follow-up).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/persona/loader.go:64 fallback removed; missing-config error message points at ddx init. 2. cli/internal/registry/audit.go:104,336,339 no longer ExpandHome; paths resolve relative to projectRoot. 3. TestUninstall_StaleHomeEntriesAreNoOp passes. 4. cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:746-759 pluginRootCandidates removed; affected resolver tests updated and passing. 5. cli/cmd/update.go threads Options{Force: true} for --force path. TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks passes. 6. TestExecuteBead_WorktreeFromPreMigrationCommit exists and passes. resolveBrokenSkillLink removed if test passes without it; otherwise kept with comment. Decision documented in commit message. 7. Doctor exits non-zero with the specified stderr substrings when legacy symlinks present. TestDoctor_FlagsLegacySymlinks passes. 8. cd cli && go build ./... && go test ./... passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:cli, area:agent, area:registry, kind:refactor, spec:FEAT-015</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-3562c48c" spec-id="FEAT-015" base-rev="aa7b31785a4479f41a410db723e62de6353b9f64" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260429T145643-841a55ca"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-015" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md">Feature: DDx Installation Architecture</ref>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260429T145643-841a55ca/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `12fbf6dd`. AC walkthrough:

1. ✅ `persona/loader.go` fallback removed; missing-config error mentions `ddx init`.
2. ✅ `registry/audit.go` ExpandHome dropped; paths resolve via `resolveAuditPath` against `projectRoot`.
3. ✅ `TestUninstall_StaleHomeEntriesAreNoOp` passes (cli/internal/registry/uninstall_test.go).
4. ✅ `pluginRootCandidates` removed from `resolver_feat008.go`; `feat008_test.go` updated to project-rooted plugin path.
5. ✅ `update.go` already routes `Force: true` through `skills.Install` via `refreshShippedSkills`; `TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks` passes (also fixed `skills.Install` pre-flight to tolerate stale leaf symlinks).
6. ✅ `TestExecuteBead_WorktreeFromPreMigrationCommit` passes without `resolveBrokenSkillLink`; fallback deleted; decision documented in commit message.
7. ✅ `TestDoctor_FlagsLegacySymlinks` passes; doctor exits non-zero with stderr substrings and `run: ddx update --force`.
8. ✅ `go build ./...` succeeds; targeted tests in persona/registry/skills/graphql/agent/cmd pass.
